pred_reason: SWC-101 (Integer Overflow and Underflow) applies due to the ceil function's underflow vulnerability when c=0. SWC-107 (Reentrancy) is valid as the transferFrom function violates checks-effects-interactions, enabling reentrancy attacks. Evidence: SafeMath.ceil function underflows with sub(c,1) when c=0; transferFrom updates allowance after state changes, violating CEI pattern

findings.Description: The transferFrom function updates allowance after other state changes, creating reentrancy vulnerability. Issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential for attackers to drain funds from user accounts with approved allowances through reentrancy attacks
findings.Location: transferFrom function (lines ~109-127), allowance update occurs after balance and total supply updates

findings.Description: The custom ceil function can underflow when c=0, causing unexpected reverts. Issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Denial-of-service attacks on token transfers, contract becomes unusable for certain values
findings.Location: SafeMath.ceil function (lines 39-43), sub(c,1) call where c could be 0

findings.Description: The findOnePercent function actually calculates 10% burn instead of intended 1%. Issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: 10x higher token burn than intended, significant financial impact on users, disrupted tokenomics
findings.Location: findOnePercent function, formula roundAmount.mul(100).div(1000) instead of amount.div(100)

findings.Description: Standard approve function is susceptible to front-running attacks when changing non-zero allowances. Issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Attackers can potentially use both old and new allowances, exceeding intended authorization limits
findings.Location: approve function (lines 97-101), uses amount parameter directly without increaseAllowance/decreaseAllowance pattern

findings.Description: transferFrom function is sensitive to front-running due to allowance/burn mechanism discrepancy. Issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential for griefing attacks, transaction failures, and manipulation of burn mechanism
findings.Location: transferFrom function, allowance deduction vs actual token transfer amount mismatch

findings.Description: Contract lacks owner functions and cannot adjust critical parameters post-deployment. Issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Complete immutability of token parameters, inability to respond to changing market conditions or fix issues
findings.Location: No owner functions implemented, oneHundredPercent variable is effectively immutable
